title: shorea longiflora
intro: Shorea longiflora, also known as Merbau, is a tropical tree species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, characterized by its distinctive wood and medicinal properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its bark and leaves exhibiting analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Shorea longiflora include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Shorea longiflora include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be made by boiling the bark and leaves in water or mixing the extracts with carrier oils.
